prose:
Method provenance block
Property: relaxed internal‐rotation barriers (ΔE‡) along all automatically detected rotatable dihedrals of n-butane, obtained from full relaxed dihedral scans.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in the xtb 6 series (called through chemkit conformational-analysis skill; density-matrix SCF converged at every scan point).
Geometry provenance: starting geometry taken from the provided butane.xyz; each scan point was fully relaxed with the ASE BFGS optimizer (fmax ≤ 0.05 eV Å⁻¹) under an exact dihedral constraint (FixInternals). This is therefore a set of GFN2-xTB relaxed scans (GFN2-xTB//GFN2-xTB).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb 6.x via chemkit (scan subcommand), ASE 3.x for optimisation.
Convergence & caveats: all 72 scan points (24 per dihedral ×3 dihedrals) converged; no engine warnings were reported. Implicit-only (gas-phase) treatment; single-conformer scans (no Boltzmann averaging).

Rotation barriers
1. CH₃–C(1)–C(2)–C(3) dihedral (terminal methyl, atoms 5-1-2-3): 2.635522 kcal mol⁻¹ (grid), 2.620546 kcal mol⁻¹ (cubic-spline interpolation).
2. C(1)–C(2)–C(3)–C(4) central C–C dihedral (atoms 1-2-3-4): 4.9233459 kcal mol⁻¹ (grid), 4.914882 kcal mol⁻¹ (interpolated).   ← headline value reported above.
3. C(2)–C(3)–C(4)–H (terminal methyl, atoms 2-3-4-12): 2.6354764 kcal mol⁻¹ (grid), 2.620510 kcal mol⁻¹ (interpolated).

Reproducibility
Live log (written during the run): /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/conformational-analysis-validation/butane/20260625-115505_butane_conformational_analysis/agent_call_01.out  (you can `tail -f` this file for the full SCF and optimizer history).  Result JSON: …/agent_call_01_live.json; scan trajectories and energy/angle plots are in the same directory.

No warnings were emitted; integrity check reports status “ok”, trustworthy = true.
